sections.name: Zimiezek
Uncertain Origins

link.alt: Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down pg. 89

sections.text: The zimiezeks believe the first of them arose from souls who were once Norgorber's allies in life who stumbled upon the god's greatest secret and were slain to conceal his identity. Some doubt this claim, given Norgorber's anathema on showing mercy. Nevertheless, a connection exists between Norgorber and both zimiezeks and karumzeks that lets the god automatically know the creatures' names and identities.

link.alt: Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down pg. 89

sections.text:
The zimiezeks, also known as those-that-weave-secrets, are one of many creatures native to Duskfathom. They resemble large spiders with humanoid hands instead of spidery feet and possess vaguely humanoid heads with multiple eyes and spider fangs for jaws. Their faces are often shrouded by a veil, mask, or webbing to both hide their identity and as a gesture of peace, for a zimiezek's bite can turn other humanoids into them.  
  
 Unlike the karumzeks, Norgorber's more common divine servitors, zimiezeks almost never leave Duskfathom. Most are average citizens who revere Norgorber as both deity and prince. Only a few serve the god of secrets directly as the city's security and elite strike force.

link.alt: Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down pg. 89
link.name: Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down pg. 89

traits.name: Rare
traits.text: This rarity indicates that a rules element is very difficult to find in the game world. A rare feat, spell, item or the like is available to players only if the GM decides to include it in the game, typically through discovery during play. Creatures with this trait are rare. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creatures is increased by 5.

traits.name: Aberration
traits.text: Aberrations are creatures from beyond the planes or corruptions of the natural order.

traits.name: Fiend
traits.text: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the unholy planes are called fiends. Fiends can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

automatic_abilities.effect: The Zimiezek gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.
automatic_abilities.name: Darting Legs
automatic_abilities.trigger: The zimiezek is targeted with an attack

automatic_abilities.name: Reactive Strike
automatic_abilities.text: The zimiezek gains two extra reactions on their turn that they can only use to make Reactive Strikes.

universal_monster_ability.effect: The monster attempts a melee Strike against the triggering creature. If the attack is a critical hit and the trigger was a manipulate action, the monster disrupts that action. This Strike doesn't count toward the monster's multiple attack penalty, and its multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.
universal_monster_ability.name: Reactive Strike
universal_monster_ability.trigger: A creature within the monster's reach uses a manipulate action or a move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Finesse
traits.text: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still calculate damage using Strength.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Reach
traits.text: This weapon can be used to attack enemies up to 10 feet away instead of only adjacent enemies. For creatures with reach, the weapon increases their reach by 5 feet.

traits.name: Versatile
traits.text: A versatile weapon can be used to deal a different type of damage than its listed type. This trait indicates the alternate damage type. For instance, a piercing weapon with versatile S can deal piercing or slashing damage. You choose the damage type each time you attack.

traits.name: Finesse
traits.text: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still calculate damage using Strength.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Range
traits.text: These attacks will either list a finite range or a range increment, which follows the normal rules for range increments.

ability.name: Web Restraints
ability.text: A creature hit by the zimiezek's web Strike is restrained either to a surface or tethered to the zimiezek until it Escapes (DC 40). The zimiezek gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Trip a tethered target and can Interact with the tether to pull a target 15 feet toward them.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.text: clumsy 2 (eight spider legs start to grow out of your back

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.text: clumsy 2, drained 1 (venomous fangs grow from your mouth, 1 day)

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.text: clumsy 2, drained 1, slowed 1 (sticky webbing exudes from your flesh, 1 day)

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.text: unconscious (you become cocooned in webbing, during which you have no need for food and water, 1 week)

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.text: death (and a brand new zimiezek emerges from your cocooned body)

traits.name: Disease
traits.text: An effect with this trait applies one or more diseases. A disease is typically an affliction.

traits.name: Virulent
traits.text: Afflictions with the virulent trait are harder to remove. You must succeed at two consecutive saves to reduce a virulent affliction’s stage by 1. A critical success reduces a virulent affliction’s stage by only 1 instead of by 2.
